title: Alchemilla madurensis
intro: Alchemilla mollis, also known as lady's mantle, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Rosaceae family, characterized by its rosettes of rounded, toothed leaves and its dense, spherical clusters of small, white or greenish-yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including menstrual disorders, wounds, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Alchemilla mollis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Alchemilla mollis is commonly used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, as well as in creams and ointments for topical application.
